title: Automate timeline start and end date based on status and date changes
excerpt: Looking for an automation for when status changes to something then set start date of timeline, and when date column changes value set end date of time line.

posts.author: rivkam (Rivka)
posts.cookedHtml: Looking for an automation for when status changes to something then set start date of timeline, and when date column changes value set end date of time line.

posts.author: Clover_Gal (Desiree McCarthy | monday.com Ambassador)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can do that with the Workflow Center. The ‘recipe’ would be ‘When Status changes to X’, then change column value (and select the timeline column)

image1480×610 41.9 KB

posts.author: ZakiImtiaz (Zaki | readylogic.co)
posts.cookedHtml:
You can absolutely set this up using Monday.com’s Custom Automations! Here’s how you can do it:
Set Timeline Start Date When Status Changes
Steps to set it up using Automation Recipe:

  • Go to your board.

  • Click on “Automate” (top right corner).

  • Choose “Create Custom Automation.”

  • Use this recipe structure:

When status changes to [Specific Value], set timeline start date to current date

Happy to help!
